metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4017
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 7: Immunogold labelling for SgII is displayed in the hippocampus with D and E: LDCVs (marked by white arrowhead) and SSV-accumulating terminal processes (marked by black arrowhead)
Figure 8: Immunogold labelling for SgII in the cerebellum with G-J: post-synaptic elements (marked by arrows), LDCVs (white arrowheads) and SSV accumulating portions (black arrowheads)
Note on Antibody used: The paper describes the antibody production protocol. cDNA fragments encoding the C terminal 26 amino acids of mouse SgII (552-577 amino acid residues, GenBank accession number BC048249) was obtained by PCR and subcloned into a thepGEX4T-2 plasmid (Amersham Bioscience, Piscataway, NJ, USA)for expression of glutathione S-transferase (GST) fusion proteins. GST fusion proteins were purified using glutathione-Sepharose 4B (Amer-sham Bioscience), and used for immunization of female New Zealand white rabbits and guinea-pigs. Antigens were emulsified with Freund's complete or incomplete adjuvant (Difco, Detroit, MI, USA) and injected subcutaneously at intervals of 2 weeks. After the sixth injection, immunoglobulins specific to antigens were affinity-purified using GST-free polypeptides coupled to cyanogen bromide-activated Sepharose 4B (Amersham Bioscience). GST-free peptides were prepared by in-column thrombin digestion of GST fusion proteins bound to glutathione-Sepharose 4B media. The specificities of these antibodies were confirmed by immunoblotting (see Fig. 2A; Supporting Information Fig. S1; Pohl et al., 1992; Alvarez-Boladoet al. , 1996). Experimental description: C57BL mice at the adult stage (about 2months of age) were used
